qualifying_org_definition: Non-profit sites that serve a useful public service such as health, social service, environmental protection, education, urban and rural development, welfare, recreation, public facilities, public safety, community service, services to aged or disabled citizens, and child care organizations.
verification_instrument: SNAP Community Service Volunteer/Workfare Verification (ABAWD Volunteer Form Fillable 11.24.25.pdf)
hours_formula_detail: Monthly household SNAP benefit divided by Maine state minimum wage ($15.10/hr as of Jan 2026). A 1-person household at max allotment (~$291/month) yields ~19 hours. Hours vary by household size and benefit level. The ABAWD Volunteer Form PDF states: 'To calculate their required hours, recipients can divide their monthly household benefit by the State minimum wage.'
accepts_outofstate_detail: Maine policy defines qualifying organizations by mission type (nonprofit serving public service purposes) with no geographic restriction on org location. Neither the ABAWD Volunteer Form, state SNAP rules, nor DHHS guidance address in-state vs. out-of-state nonprofit location. Absent an explicit state restriction, the federal default applies — any qualifying 501(c)(3) regardless of state of incorporation should be accepted operationally.
geographic_waivers: Maine had a geographic waiver covering 213 cities, towns, unorganized territories, townships, and reservations approved Sept 17, 2024, effective retroactive to Oct 1, 2024 through Sept 30, 2025 (Rule 234A). Under the One Big Beautiful Bill Act of 2025 (OBBBA), area-based waivers are now restricted to areas with unemployment exceeding 10%, a much narrower threshold. The prior waiver period ended Sept 30, 2025. As of 2026, Maine is enforcing ABAWD requirements statewide (consistent with abawd_enforced_2026=yes), though Maine DHHS may be pursuing a narrower OBBBA-compliant waiver for high-unemployment areas. No confirmed active 2026 waiver found.
submission_deadline: By the 10th day of the month following the reporting month (standard Maine SNAP change reporting rule). The ABAWD Volunteer Form also states volunteer hours must be verified at each application or annual eligibility review.
notes:
Maine is a clean Tier 1 state: the nonprofit supervisor signs the state's two-page 'SNAP Community Service Volunteer/Workfare Verification' form certifying the volunteer's name, org, months, and hours — no MOU, county assignment, EDP, or provider pre-registration is required. One operational nuance: Maine uses workfare math (household SNAP benefit divided by state minimum wage, currently $15.10) rather than a flat 80-hour standard, so the required monthly hours may be below 80; this is a calculation rule, not a structural hoop. Volunteer hours must be reverified at each application or annual eligibility review.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted targeted adversarial searches to attempt to refute the Tier 1 clean classification. Searched for: (1) mandatory FSET/E&T enrollment as a prestep to volunteer hours, (2) an approved volunteer site list requiring org pre-registration, (3) EDP or case manager assignment requirements, (4) new procedural gates introduced by Maine's 2025 rule updates (Rules 240A and 240P implementing Budget Reconciliation Act changes). No refutation found. Key findings: (a) The Maine DHHS applications-forms page lists only two ABAWD-related forms — the standalone volunteer verification form and a medical exemption form — confirming no separate work registration or enrollment form exists. (b) Maine Equal Justice (anti-hunger legal aid org) describes volunteering as a direct compliance path with no prior administrative enrollment step. (c) Maine does make FSET participation mandatory for ABAWDs residing near the four Career Center sites (Augusta, Bangor, Lewiston, Portland), but this applies to FSET program enrollment specifically; all authoritative sources consistently frame volunteer community service as a parallel and independent compliance path under 7 CFR §273.24(a)(2)(iii) — FSET enrollment is not required before volunteer hours count. The FSET service-providers page lists only E&T program contractors, not volunteer host sites, and describes no MOU or pre-approval process for nonprofit volunteer hosts. (d) Rules 240A and 240P made routine technical changes (age expansion, terminology, minimum wage calculations) with no new procedural gates on the volunteer lane. county_variation_flag remains false because the FSET mandatory-in-four-counties provision governs FSET enrollment, not the volunteer lane. remote_stance remains unknown — no source addressed whether online/remote volunteer work qualifies; this is a gap for operational guidance but does not affect the tier. Confidence set to high: the combination of DHHS forms page (no work registration form), Maine Equal Justice guidance (no prestep), Rule 240A text (volunteer lane defined independently), and absence of any approved-site list across multiple targeted searches provides multi-source confirmation that no hidden administrative hoop blocks the general volunteer lane.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) HOURS FORMULA: Confirmed workfare formula from the ABAWD Volunteer Form PDF itself, which states 'recipients can divide their monthly household benefit by the State minimum wage.' Maine minimum wage is $15.10/hr as of Jan 2026. Representative 1-person household (~$291 max allotment) yields ~19 hours/month. Set monthly_hours_required=19 as a representative figure; actual hours vary by household. (2) OUT-OF-STATE NONPROFIT: Policy is silent — qualifying org definition is mission-based only (nonprofit serving public service), with no in-state location requirement stated anywhere in the form, rules, or DHHS guidance. Federal default (any qualifying nonprofit) applies. (3) GEOGRAPHIC WAIVERS: Maine's prior waiver covering 213 localities (Rule 234A, effective Oct 1 2024–Sept 30 2025) has expired. OBBBA restricts new waivers to areas with >10% unemployment; Maine's enforcement status (abawd_enforced_2026=yes) reflects this. (4) SUBMISSION UX: The ABAWD Volunteer Form explicitly lists three submission methods — mail to Farmington DHHS office, email to Farmington.DHHS@Maine.gov, or upload to MyMaineConnection.gov portal. Reporting cadence is monthly; deadline is 10th of the following month per Maine's SNAP change reporting rules. (5) FORM FIELDS: Extracted directly from the fillable PDF: Name of Volunteer, Name of Organization, Month(s), Volunteer Hours, Signature, Title, Date, Phone Number. Maps to: org_name, org_phone, org_signature, supervisor_name (title line), recipient_name, hours_total, period_start_date/period_end_date. No EIN, notarization, case number, DOB, or address required on the form.
